title: Salty Gooey Venezulan Vine Leaves Empanadas
description: A traditional Venezuelan dish featuring crispy empanadas filled with a tangy mix of vine leaves, pickled cucumbers, and gooey cheese.

ingredients.name: vine leaves (fresh or frozen)
ingredients.notes: You can also use collard green leaves as a substitute.

ingredients.name: pickled cucumber
ingredients.notes: Use store-bought or homemade pickling liquid.

ingredients.name: low-fat mozzarella cheese
ingredients.notes: You can also use other low-fat cheeses like cheddar or feta.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: Red onion adds a sweet flavor, but use any variety you like.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste, and be careful not to burn the garlic.

ingredients.name: cumin
ingredients.notes: This adds a traditional Venezuelan flavor.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher or sea salt for a coarser texture.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral oil for frying.

ingredients.name: flour
ingredients.notes: Use all-purpose flour or a low-carb alternative like almond flour.

ingredients.name: egg
ingredients.notes: Use as an egg wash for the empanada dough.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: Use to make the empanada dough and as a brushing liquid.

instructions: Preheat the oil in a large skillet or deep fryer to 350°F (175°C).
In a large bowl, combine the chopped onion, minced garlic, cumin, and salt. Mix well.
Add the shredded mozzarella cheese to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Add the sliced pickled cucumber and chopped vine leaves to the bowl. Mix gently, being careful not to tear the leaves.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour, egg, and water to make the empanada dough.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and flatten slightly into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of the filling mixture onto one half of each dough disk, leaving a 1/2-inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Dip the sealed empanadas into the vegetable oil and fry until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Drain the empanadas on paper towels and serve hot.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
